examples.title: Binary Tree Node Structure
examples.description: Definition of a node structure for binary tree with left and right child pointers
examples.code:
STRUCTURE TreeNode
  DATA: value
  POINTER: left  // Points to left child
  POINTER: right // Points to right child
END STRUCTURE

// Creating a new node
FUNCTION createNode(value)
  newNode ← ALLOCATE(TreeNode)
  newNode.value ← value
  newNode.left ← NULL
  newNode.right ← NULL
  RETURN newNode
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Each node contains data and two pointers - one for left child and one for right child. NULL indicates absence of child.

examples.title: BST Insertion Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to insert a new node maintaining BST property
examples.code:
FUNCTION insert(root, value)
  // Base case: found position to insert
  IF root = NULL THEN
    RETURN createNode(value)
  END IF
  
  // Recursively find correct position
  IF value < root.value THEN
    root.left ← insert(root.left, value)
  ELSE THEN
    root.right ← insert(root.right, value)
  END IF
  
  RETURN root
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Compares value with current node, goes left if smaller, right if larger, until NULL position found.

examples.title: Tree Traversal Algorithms
examples.description: Preorder, Inorder and Postorder traversals of binary tree
examples.code:
// Preorder: Root, Left, Right
FUNCTION preorder(node)
  IF node ≠ NULL THEN
    VISIT(node)
    preorder(node.left)
    preorder(node.right)
  END IF
END FUNCTION

// Inorder: Left, Root, Right
FUNCTION inorder(node)
  IF node ≠ NULL THEN
    inorder(node.left)
    VISIT(node)
    inorder(node.right)
  END IF
END FUNCTION

// Postorder: Left, Right, Root
FUNCTION postorder(node)
  IF node ≠ NULL THEN
    postorder(node.left)
    postorder(node.right)
    VISIT(node)
  END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Three depth-first traversal methods differing in order of visiting root relative to children. Used for different applications.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of nodes

examples.title: Level Order Traversal (BFS)
examples.description: Breadth-First Search traversal using queue data structure
examples.explanation: Uses queue to process nodes level by level. Nodes at current level are processed before moving to next level.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is max width of tree
